title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Arkansas
description: This race is for Arkansas’s Class II seat in the U.S. Senate, with incumbent Republican Tom Cotton seeking another term in 2026. The contest is one of the Senate races that will help determine chamber control, though Arkansas is generally rated as a safe Republican state. The general election is expected to favor Cotton, while Democrat Hallie Shoffner and Libertarian Jeff Wadlin are also on the ballot. The main contrast is between Cotton’s national-security and conservative record and Shoffner’s campaign emphasis on farming, working families, and economic relief.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-04.

candidates.name: Tom Cotton
candidates.summary: Tom Cotton is the incumbent U.S. senator from Arkansas, first elected in 2014, and is seeking reelection in 2026. A former Army officer and House member, he has built his Senate profile around national security, conservative policy positions, and representing Arkansas in Washington.

summary_sources.title: Biographical Directory of the United States Congress: Tom Cotton

career_history.title: Member of the U.S. House of Representatives
career_history.description: Served one term in Congress before winning the Senate seat.

career_history.title: Army Infantry Officer
career_history.description: Served on active duty, including combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan.

career_history.title: Management Consultant
career_history.description: Worked in consulting after military service and before his election to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2012.

candidates.donor_summary: Tom Cotton’s campaign finance profile shows a large federal fundraising operation. OpenSecrets lists his top industry as retired donors and his top disclosed contributor as Apollo Global Management at $90,500, with securities & investment, Republican/conservative, real estate, and lawyers/law firms also among the leading industries. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Tom Cotton is an incumbent U.S. senator, so he has an extensive legislative voting record. GovTrack shows he has served since 2015, missed 87 of 4,182 roll call votes (2.1%), and has generally voted in line with Republican priorities; one highlighted key vote on GovTrack is his yea vote on the Continuing Appropriations and Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2023. Confidence: high.

links.title: Sen. Tom Cotton - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets
Biographical Directory of the United States Congress: Tom Cotton

Economy.stance: Tom Cotton says he wants to lower costs for working Arkansans by cutting taxes, expanding domestic energy production, and limiting inflationary spending. He has also backed legislation and Senate actions aimed at reducing energy costs and opposing broad spending increases.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate
Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Energy Costs for Arkansans

Education.stance: Tom Cotton supports local control of schools, parental rights, and school choice, and he has opposed what he describes as ideological content in classrooms. He has also backed legislation targeting federal funding for the 1619 Project and other curriculum-related initiatives.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate
Cotton Bill to Defund 1619 Project Curriculum

Healthcare.stance: Tom Cotton supports protections for people with pre-existing conditions and says he favors a market-based health care system rather than a government-run model. He has criticized the Affordable Care Act and backed Republican efforts to replace or revise it.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate
Senators Introduce the Protect Act to Ensure Protections and Affordable Coverage for Americans with Pre-Existing Conditions
Cotton Statement on the American Health Care Act

Climate/Energy.stance: Tom Cotton supports expanding domestic energy production and has opposed renewable-energy mandates he says could raise electricity costs. He has introduced legislation to limit state renewable mandates and to lower energy prices for Arkansans.

sources.title: Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Arkansas Energy Prices
Cotton proposes blocking states from mandating renewable energy

Tech & AI.stance: Tom Cotton has emphasized U.S. AI leadership as a national-security issue and has backed federal efforts to set uniform AI standards and restrict foreign-made AI and robotics in sensitive federal uses. He has also urged stronger controls on advanced chip exports and data security.

sources.title: Cotton to Lutnick: Now is the Time to Preserve American AI Dominance
Cotton, Huizenga to Lutnick: Stronger Export Controls of AI Chips is Needed
Cotton-Schumer Introduce Bipartisan Bill to Protect Americans' Data from Foreign Adversaries

Immigration.stance: Tom Cotton supports stricter immigration enforcement, tighter asylum standards, and measures to reduce illegal immigration. He has also backed proposals to reform legal immigration and to limit access to federal benefits and banking for undocumented immigrants.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate
Cotton Introduces Bill to Enforce Stricter Asylum Standards
Cotton Introduces Bill to Put American Citizens First
Cotton Introduces Bill to Ban Illegal Migrants from American Banking System

Foreign Policy.stance: Tom Cotton advocates a hawkish foreign policy centered on military strength, deterrence, and a hard line toward adversaries such as China and Iran. He has repeatedly argued for robust defense spending and a more aggressive national-security posture.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate
Cotton Details Afghanistan Rescue Effort
Senator Tom Cotton on U.S. Foreign Policy | C-SPAN

Social Justice.stance: Tom Cotton has opposed critical race theory and many diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives, arguing they are divisive and should not receive federal support. He has introduced legislation to restrict federal funding for CRT-related instruction and DEI bureaucracies.

sources.title: Cotton Introduces Bill to Defund Critical Race Theory
Cotton, Colleagues Introduce Bills to Stop Teaching of Critical Race Theory in Schools and Military Institutions
Cotton, Banks Introduce Legislation to Cap Compensation to DEI Bureaucrats

Election Reform.stance: Tom Cotton supports voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements for federal elections and has backed the SAVE Act. He has also criticized broader federal election-overhaul proposals such as the For the People Act.

sources.title: Floor Speech on SAVE America Act
The 'For the People Act' Has Nothing to Offer Our Country
S.128 - SAVE Act (All Info)

Local Issues.stance: Tom Cotton’s Arkansas-focused messaging emphasizes lower taxes, energy affordability, school choice, parental rights in education, and support for law enforcement. His campaign frames these as priorities for Arkansas families and local communities.

sources.title: Issues | Tom Cotton for U.S. Senate

Reproductive Rights.stance: Tom Cotton describes himself as pro-life and has supported legislation to restrict abortion, including measures related to 20-week abortion limits and born-alive protections. He has also opposed federal abortion-rights expansions and criticized abortion providers and related funding.

sources.title: Cotton Speaks on the Senate Floor in Support of Pro-Life Legislation
Cotton Statement on the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act Vote
Cotton Statement on the Anniversary of Roe v. Wade

Guns & Safety.stance: Tom Cotton generally supports gun rights and has opposed broad gun-control measures, while backing tougher criminal penalties and a law-and-order approach to public safety. He has also supported some targeted measures such as allowing concealed carry for certain federal officials.

sources.title: Cotton, Blackburn Introduce Bill to Allow Concealed Carry for Prosecutors, Federal Judges
Cotton Statement on the Senate's Votes on Gun Control Legislation
President Trump and Republicans are Restoring Law and Order

candidates.name: Hallie Shoffner
candidates.summary: Hallie Shoffner is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Arkansas and a sixth-generation farmer and small-business owner from the Arkansas Delta. Her campaign emphasizes lowering costs for working families, supporting farmers and rural communities, and expanding access to healthcare, education, and basic services.

career_history.title: Farmer / Farm operator
career_history.description: Operated the family farm in the Arkansas Delta and worked in agriculture.

career_history.title: Conservation and sustainable agriculture advocate
career_history.description: Worked to advance conservation, sustainable farming, and regional food production.

career_history.title: Board member
career_history.description: Serves on the board of the Arkansas Foodbank.

candidates.donor_summary: Hallie Shoffner’s campaign is a newer federal committee with $1,237,490.01 in total receipts from 2025-07-01 through 2026-02-11 and $503,970.22 cash on hand. The FEC summary shows almost all receipts came from individual contributions ($1,233,936.91), with only $3,510.00 from other committees and no party committee money reported; public FEC data does not yet show a dominant industry donor base. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Hallie Shoffner is a candidate for U.S. Senate and does not have a legislative voting record. Publicly available materials found in this search identify her as a Democratic candidate and farmer/business owner, but no roll-call voting history or comparable legislative record was located. Confidence: high.

links.title: HALLIE SHOFFNER FOR ARKANSAS - committee overview | FEC
Arkansas farmer challenges Cotton for U.S. Senate seat

Economy.stance: Shoffner says Arkansas needs an economy that lowers costs for working families, raises the minimum wage, expands workforce training, cuts taxes for working families, supports local businesses, and modernizes infrastructure. She also says she wants to keep jobs in Arkansas and strengthen unions.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Education.stance: Shoffner supports higher pay for teachers and school staff, along with more investment in workforce and technical education, apprenticeships, and partnerships with community colleges. She says education should lead to good-paying jobs and stronger local opportunity.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Healthcare.stance: Shoffner says she would make healthcare more affordable and accessible by protecting the Affordable Care Act, strengthening Medicare and Medicaid, lowering prescription drug prices, and cutting insurance red tape. She says access to care should not depend on income or ZIP code.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Reproductive Rights.stance: No standalone abortion policy was found in public campaign materials. Her campaign instead emphasizes support for mothers and families through affordable childcare, paid leave, quality healthcare, and access to lifesaving medical care, so this summary reflects adjacent family and health policy language rather than a direct abortion position.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Immigration.stance: Shoffner has said the immigration system needs comprehensive reform and bipartisan cooperation, with a focus on fixing the system rather than relying only on broad enforcement or border expansion. Her comments emphasize collaboration and practical solutions.

sources.title: Issue Spotlight: Where Arkansas’s U.S. Senate candidates stand on immigration policy
Issue Spotlight: Where Arkansas’s U.S. Senate candidates stand on immigration policy

Guns & Safety.stance: Shoffner has not published a dedicated firearms platform. Her public safety messaging focuses on supporting law enforcement and first responders, investing in community-based crime prevention, and addressing domestic violence, human trafficking, addiction, and crimes against children.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Social Justice.stance: Shoffner frames fairness as a core theme of her campaign, saying she wants stronger worker protections, support for unions, affordable healthcare, food security, and stronger safety nets such as Social Security and Medicare. Her messaging emphasizes dignity and opportunity for working Arkansans.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities

Local Issues.stance: Shoffner’s local agenda centers on lowering the cost of living, supporting farmers and rural communities, strengthening local food systems, improving healthcare access, and investing in education and infrastructure. She says these priorities are aimed at making Arkansas a place where hard work leads to a good life.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities
Meet Hallie

Climate/Energy.stance: Shoffner has said she supports environmentally friendly farm practices and has described climate change as affecting farmers, but she has not published a detailed climate or energy platform on her campaign site. Her public priorities focus more on agriculture, infrastructure, and lowering costs than on specific energy policy proposals.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner returns to her roots; pushes for environmentally friendly farm practices
Meet the Arkansas farmer trying to adapt to the changing climate
Hallie’s Priorities

candidates.name: Jeff Wadlin
candidates.summary: Jeff Wadlin is the Libertarian nominee for the U.S. Senate in Arkansas in 2026. Public campaign materials describe him as running to unify Arkansans, reduce partisan conflict, and give people more power at the federal level.

summary_sources.title: Libertarian Party of Arkansas nominates 2026 general election candidates

candidates.donor_summary: Jeff Wadlin’s federal committee was newly registered, and the accessible FEC committee overview did not yet show a public financial summary with receipts or top contributors at the time reviewed. The profile therefore does not identify any disclosed donor industries or major contributors yet. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Jeff Wadlin is a candidate for U.S. Senate and does not have a legislative voting record. Public materials found in this search identify him as the Libertarian nominee and provide campaign messaging, but no roll-call voting history or comparable legislative record was located. Confidence: high.

links.title: WADLIN FOR SENATE 2026 - committee overview | FEC

Economy.stance: No public position found beyond general campaign messaging about reducing federal power and supporting individual freedom.

flags.concern: The election date is listed as 2026-11-03 (a Tuesday), which is plausible for a midterm general election. However, midterm general elections in 2026 would fall on November 3, 2026, which is indeed a Tuesday — this appears correct. No flag needed on the date itself, but the primary date is not mentioned anywhere in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding the primary election date for completeness, as Arkansas holds primaries before the general election.

flags.concern: Tom Cotton's Harvard A.B. is listed as 1998. My training data suggests Cotton graduated from Harvard in 1999, but the profile cites official sources (Ballotpedia, bioguide.congress.gov, and cotton.senate.gov/about). The cited sources should be treated as authoritative — a human reviewer should spot-check the graduation year against the Biographical Directory of Congress entry.
flags.suggestion: Verify the 1998 graduation year against the cited Biographical Directory of Congress source (https://bioguide.congress.gov/search/bio/C001095).

flags.concern: Cotton's Army service is listed as 2005–2010. My training data suggests he was commissioned in 2004 and served approximately 2004–2009, but the profile cites official sources. Cannot independently confirm these exact dates from training data — human verification against the cited official sources is advisable.
flags.suggestion: Verify Army service dates against the official Senate biography at https://www.cotton.senate.gov/about or the Biographical Directory entry.

flags.concern: The voting summary highlights Cotton's 'yea vote on the Continuing Appropriations and Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2023' as a key GovTrack vote. My training data suggests Cotton was a consistent skeptic of Ukraine aid packages. This specific vote claim is sourced to GovTrack and should be presumed accurate, but it is unusual enough that a human reviewer should confirm it against the GovTrack page.
flags.suggestion: Spot-check the referenced vote on https://www.govtrack.us/congress/members/tom_cotton/412508 to confirm Cotton voted yea on this specific measure.

flags.concern: Shoffner's undergraduate field of study at Vanderbilt is listed as 'Unknown.' The profile does not cite a source confirming attendance at Vanderbilt, and no degree year is provided. The claim is unverified and unsourced.
flags.suggestion: Add a source for Shoffner's Vanderbilt attendance (e.g., her campaign bio or LinkedIn profile which is already linked) and populate the field if it can be confirmed.

flags.concern: The profile notes 'medium' confidence and acknowledges no standalone abortion position was found. This is handled transparently. However, given that this is a contested Senate race and reproductive rights is a salient issue in Arkansas post-Dobbs, it may be worth a deeper search of news coverage and candidate interviews for any stated position.
flags.suggestion: Search Arkansas news archives and any candidate forums or debates for Shoffner's stated position on reproductive rights, which may exist in interview or debate form rather than on her campaign website.

flags.concern: The X (Twitter) value for Shoffner is a plain string ('https://x.com/hallieshoffner') rather than an array like the other social media fields. This is an internal formatting inconsistency — all other social media entries use arrays.
flags.suggestion: Wrap the X URL in an array: ["https://x.com/hallieshoffner"] for consistency with the schema used elsewhere.

flags.concern: Jeff Wadlin's career history is entirely empty. While he is a lower-profile candidate, the profile includes a LinkedIn URL that may contain professional background information.
flags.suggestion: Check the linked LinkedIn profile (https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeffwadlin) for career information that could be added.

flags.concern: Jeff Wadlin's education section is entirely empty with no sourced attempt to locate educational background.
flags.suggestion: Check Ballotpedia and LinkedIn for any educational background. If none is publicly available, add a note indicating it was not found rather than leaving the array empty.

reviews.summary: This is a well-constructed, thoroughly sourced candidate profile for the 2026 Arkansas Senate race. Tom Cotton's section is exemplary: career history, education, and policy positions are all backed by multiple credible sources including official Senate press releases, GovTrack, OpenSecrets, FEC filings, Ballotpedia, and the Biographical Directory of Congress. Issue stances are specific, consistent with sourced materials, and written in neutral, factual language. Hallie Shoffner's profile appropriately relies on her official campaign site, FEC filings, Arkansas news outlets, and external feature reporting; the profile is transparent about gaps (e.g., reproductive rights, foreign policy) with honest medium/low-confidence labels. Jeff Wadlin's section correctly acknowledges limited public information given his lower-profile candidacy, with sourced acknowledgment of ballot access. The profile's handling of uncertainty — flagging low-confidence stances rather than inventing positions — is a genuine strength. Minor issues noted below are largely informational and do not undermine the overall accuracy or fairness of the profile.

flags.concern: The field of study for the Vanderbilt University degree is listed as 'Unknown' and years are missing for both degrees.
flags.suggestion: If this data becomes available on the official campaign 'About' page or LinkedIn, it should be updated to complete the biographical record.

flags.concern: The agent correctly identifies the lack of a standalone abortion policy but helpfully provides context on 'adjacent' family and health policies. This is a good example of nuanced reporting.

reviews.summary: This profile is exceptionally well-researched and internally consistent. It provides deep sourcing for the incumbent, including specific legislative actions and campaign finance data from OpenSecrets and the FEC. The challenger profiles are handled with professional neutrality, clearly distinguishing between stated platforms and 'no public position' gaps where information is unavailable. The use of local Arkansas news sources and official government records provides a high level of confidence.

flags.concern: The stance on reproductive rights for Hallie Shoffner is marked as medium confidence and does not directly address abortion policy, instead focusing on adjacent family and health policies. While this is supported by the cited source, it may leave readers unclear on a key issue.
flags.suggestion: If possible, search for additional statements or news coverage directly addressing reproductive rights or abortion policy to strengthen this section.

flags.concern: The stance on climate and energy for Hallie Shoffner is marked as medium confidence and lacks a detailed policy platform, focusing more on agriculture and general environmental concerns. The cited sources support this, but the position remains somewhat vague.
flags.suggestion: Consider searching for more specific statements or policy proposals on climate or energy to enhance clarity, if available.

flags.concern: For Jeff Wadlin, most issue stances are marked as 'no public position found' with low confidence. While this is appropriately documented with sources indicating a search was conducted, the lack of detailed positions limits the depth of the profile for this candidate.
flags.suggestion: Continue monitoring for updates to campaign materials or news coverage that might provide more specific issue stances for Jeff Wadlin.

flags.concern: Jeff Wadlin's career history and education sections are empty, with no data provided. While this may reflect a lack of public information and is not necessarily an error given the obscurity of the candidate, it leaves a gap in the profile.
flags.suggestion: If possible, search for additional biographical information through LinkedIn, news articles, or other public records to fill this gap.

reviews.summary: This profile for the 2026 U.S. Senate election in Arkansas is exceptionally well-researched and comprehensive. It provides detailed, well-sourced information on all three candidates—Tom Cotton, Hallie Shoffner, and Jeff Wadlin—with high-confidence stances on most issues for the major candidates, backed by credible sources such as official campaign websites, government records, and reputable news outlets. The language remains neutral and nonpartisan throughout, and gaps in information (especially for Jeff Wadlin) are appropriately documented with evidence of a good-faith search. Candidate backgrounds are consistent with cited sources, and the profile demonstrates strong coverage effort across a wide range of issues.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 92/100.
